title: Distributed In-Memory Cache Service
architecture: Decentralized consensus cluster using Raft Protocol, peer-to-peer gossip discovery, and an LRU cache engine with TTL eviction.
problem: High-latency page renders and database CPU bottlenecks during flash sale write-spikes.
solution: Built a Golang-based distributed cache service supporting sub-millisecond lookups, cluster auto-discovery, and eventual consistency sync.
challenges: Handling split-brain consensus failures and re-balancing key hashes upon dynamic node terminations.
lessonsLearned: Learned the trade-offs between linearizability and availability in high-throughput network programming.
futureImprovements: Plan to add a WebAssembly admin dashboard and support hot-swapping consistency profiles.

title: Headless WordPress Rendering Core
architecture: Static-first decoupled rendering client querying a Dockerized WordPress instance via optimized GraphQL schemas, cached at edge CDN worker routes.
problem: Slow page loading speeds and high security exposure on shared hosting WordPress servers.
solution: Designed a Next.js static rendering pipeline querying GraphQL APIs, decreasing load times by 75% and removing public server access interfaces.
challenges: Synchronizing page rebuild triggers instantly when editors publish post changes in the WordPress dashboard.
lessonsLearned: Discovered patterns for mapping standard Gutenberg block layouts into highly semantic dynamic React wrappers.
futureImprovements: Plan to build a custom Gutenberg block plugin that auto-compiles blocks into headless schema queries.
